Title: Submit basic details with valid data
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to /basic-details page | 2. Form is displayed and empty
Steps: 1. Enter valid name in "Full Name" field | 2. Select a valid date of birth (18+ years old) | 3. Enter complete address in "Address" field | 4. Click "Submit Details" button | 5. Wait for submission to complete
Test Data: Name: John Doe | Date of Birth: 1990-05-15 | Address: 123 Main Street, Apartment 4B, New York, NY 10001
Expected Result: Form submits successfully | Success message "User details submitted successfully!" is displayed | Form fields are cleared | New user appears in the table below | Database record is created

Title: Complete user journey from landing page to basic details submission
Preconditions: 1. Backend server is running on port 5000 | 2. Database is initialized with basic_details table | 3. Frontend is running on port 3000
Steps: 1. Open landing page at http://localhost:3000 | 2. Click "Basic Details" link in navigation bar | 3. Verify Basic Details page loads | 4. Fill in all form fields with valid data | 5. Submit the form | 6. Verify success message appears | 7. Verify user appears in table below | 8. Navigate back to home page | 9. Return to Basic Details page | 10. Verify submitted user persists in table
Test Data: Name: Jane Smith | Date of Birth: 1985-08-22 | Address: 456 Oak Avenue, Building C, Los Angeles, CA 90001
Expected Result: User can navigate from landing page to Basic Details page | Form submission succeeds | Data persists across page refreshes | Navigation links work correctly | User data is visible in the table

Title: Submit form with minimum valid address length (exactly 10 characters)
Steps: 1. Enter valid name | 2. Select valid date of birth | 3. Enter address with exactly 10 characters | 4. Click submit
Test Data: Name: Bob Lee | Date of Birth: 1995-03-10 | Address: 12 Main St
Expected Result: Form accepts 10-character address as valid | Submission succeeds | User appears in table with complete address

Title: Submit form with user exactly 18 years old (minimum age)
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to /basic-details page | 2. Calculate date for exactly 18 years ago from today
Steps: 1. Enter valid name | 2. Select date of birth exactly 18 years ago | 3. Enter valid address | 4. Click submit
Test Data: Name: Alice Young | Date of Birth: 2008-04-28 | Address: 789 Elm Street, Boston, MA 02101
Expected Result: Form accepts 18-year-old user | Validation passes | Submission succeeds

Title: Delete user from table
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to /basic-details page | 2. At least one user exists in the table
Steps: 1. Locate user in the table | 2. Click delete button (trash icon) for that user | 3. Confirm deletion in the browser confirmation dialog | 4. Wait for table to refresh
Test Data: User: Any existing user in table
Expected Result: Browser confirmation dialog appears | After confirmation, user is removed from table | Table refreshes automatically | User is deleted from database

Title: Submit multiple users in sequence
Steps: 1. Submit first user with valid data | 2. Wait for success message | 3. Submit second user with different valid data | 4. Wait for success message | 5. Submit third user with different valid data | 6. Verify all three users appear in table
Test Data: User 1: John A | User 2: Jane B | User 3: Bob C | All with valid DOBs and addresses
Expected Result: All three submissions succeed | All users appear in table in reverse chronological order (newest first) | Each submission clears the form | Success message appears after each submission

Title: View user list after page refresh
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to /basic-details page | 2. Submit at least 2 users | 3. Verify users appear in table
Steps: 1. Refresh the browser page (F5 or Ctrl+R) | 2. Wait for page to load | 3. Verify table section
Test Data: Users exist in database from precondition
Expected Result: Page loads successfully | All previously submitted users appear in table | Data persists after refresh | Users are displayed in correct order (newest first)

Title: Submit form with empty name field
Steps: 1. Leave "Full Name" field empty | 2. Enter valid date of birth | 3. Enter valid address | 4. Click submit button
Test Data: Name: (empty) | Date of Birth: 1990-01-01 | Address: 123 Test Street, City
Expected Result: Error message "Name is required" appears below name field | Name field is highlighted with error styling | Form does not submit | No API call is made

Title: Submit form with name less than 2 characters

Expected Result: Error message "Name must be at least 2 characters" appears | Name field shows error styling | Form does not submit

Title: Attempt to delete user but cancel confirmation
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to /basic-details page | 2. At least one user exists in table
Steps: 1. Click delete button for a user | 2. When confirmation dialog appears, click "Cancel"
Test Data: User: Any existing user
Expected Result: Confirmation dialog is dismissed | User remains in table | No deletion occurs | No API call is made

Title: Backend server is down during submission
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to /basic-details page | 2. Stop backend server on port 5000
Steps: 1. Enter valid form data | 2. Click submit | 3. Wait for response
Test Data: Valid user data
Expected Result: Error message "Network error. Please try again." appears | Form data remains in fields (not cleared) | User can retry submission | No database record is created

Title: Submit form with very long name (255 characters)
Steps: 1. Enter name with exactly 255 characters | 2. Enter valid DOB | 3. Enter valid address | 4. Submit form
Test Data: Name: (255 character string) | Date of Birth: 1990-01-01 | Address: Valid address
Expected Result: Form accepts 255-character name | Submission succeeds | Full name is stored and displayed in table

Title: API endpoint GET /api/basic-details returns all users
Preconditions: 1. Database has 3 users | 2. Backend server is running
Steps: 1. Make GET request to http://localhost:5000/api/basic-details | 2. Verify response
Test Data: N/A
Expected Result: Response status is 200 | Response contains success: true | Response data array contains all 3 users | Users are ordered by created_at DESC | Pagination info is included

Title: API endpoint POST /api/basic-details creates user
Preconditions: 1. Backend server is running | 2. Database is accessible
Steps: 1. Make POST request to http://localhost:5000/api/basic-details with valid JSON body | 2. Verify response | 3. Query database to confirm
Test Data: JSON: {"name": "API Test User", "dateOfBirth": "1988-06-15", "address": "API Test Address 123"}
Expected Result: Response status is 201 | Response contains success: true | Response includes created user data | User is inserted into basic_details table | created_at timestamp is set automatically
